stem: The floor of an auditorium has an area of 720 square meters. An architect creates a scale model of the floor of the auditorium, where the length of each side of the model is 1/12 times the length of the corresponding side of the actual floor of the auditorium. What is the area, in square meters, of the scale model?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. When linear dimensions are scaled by 1/12, areas are scaled by (1/12)² = 1/144. The model's area is 720 × 1/144 = 5 square meters.

Choice A scales by 1/12 instead of 1/144. Choice B gives the linear scale factor's denominator. Choice C does not scale at all.
